claims.claim: - url: https://doi.org/10.1177/02698811231211219 - file_origin: pdf - review_state: extracted --- ## Summary This systematic review evaluates drug–drug interactions involving classic psychedelics, including LSD, psilocybin, mescaline, DMT, and ayahuasca.
claims.notes: Extracted from Halman-et-al-2023-Drug-drug-interactions-involving-classic-psychedelics-a-systematic-review using rule-based keyword matching.

claims.claim: It identifies pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interactions across multiple drug classes, including antidepressants, antipsychotics, and MAOIs.
claims.notes: Extracted from Halman-et-al-2023-Drug-drug-interactions-involving-classic-psychedelics-a-systematic-review using rule-based keyword matching.

claims.claim: P-gp) --- ## Clinical / Practical Guidance - Avoid combining psychedelics with strong serotonergic medications without supervision - Expect attenuation of effects with SSRIs or antipsychotics - Monitor cardiovascular and psychological effects --- ## Contraindications / Risks - Potential serotonin toxicity - Altered drug metabolism - Unpredictable pharmacodynamic effects - Limited safety data in polypharmacy --- ## Interaction Relevance - Psychedelics + antidepressants → attenuation or modulation - Psychedelics + antipsychotics → receptor-level antagonism - DMT + MAOI → pharmacokinetic activation - Psychedelics + CYP inhibitors → altered metabolism --- ## Limitations - Limited number of human studies - High heterogeneity - Reliance on case reports - Lack of controlled DDI trials --- ## Notes Critical Tier 4 source for dataset interaction modeling.
claims.notes: Extracted from Halman-et-al-2023-Drug-drug-interactions-involving-classic-psychedelics-a-systematic-review using rule-based keyword matching.
